2798.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ used to stand on the Day of Friday leaning against a palm tree trunk. A woman from the Ansar said: O Messenger of Allah, shall we make for you something to sit on? He said: As you wish. She had a pulpit made for him. When it was the next Friday, he sat on the pulpit. The palm trunk cried out like a young camel until the Prophet ﷺ descended and embraced it, and it fell silent.
2798.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa berdiri pada hari Jumat bersandar pada batang pohon kurma. Seorang wanita dari Anshar berkata: Ya Rasulullah, bolehkah kami membuat sesuatu untukmu agar kamu bisa duduk? Beliau berkata: Terserahmu. Ia membuat mimbar untuk beliau. Ketika Jumat berikutnya, beliau duduk di atas mimbar. Batang kurma itu menangis seperti unta muda hingga Nabi ﷺ turun dan memeluknya, lalu batang itu diam.

2799.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood up on Friday, the trunk of a palm tree would groan — toward which he used to lean — and kept groaning until the pulpit was placed for him. When the pulpit was made, the trunk ceased its groaning.'
2799.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri pada hari Jumat, batang pohon kurma — tempat beliau biasa bersandar — berderit, dan terus berderit hingga mimbar disiapkan untuknya. Ketika mimbar dibuat, batang itu berhenti berderit.'

2800.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that Abu Talhah ordered Umm Sulaym (may Allah be pleased with her) to make something for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to sit on. She made a cushion for him and he sat on it.
2800.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Abu Thalhah memerintahkan Umm Sulaym (radhiyallahu 'anha) untuk membuat sesuatu bagi Rasulullah ﷺ agar beliau bisa duduk. Ia membuat bantal untuknya dan beliau duduk di atasnya.

2801.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Ubayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he cooked a pot of food for the Prophet ﷺ. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Hand me the foreleg.' He did so. He said: 'Hand me another foreleg.' He said: 'O Messenger of Allah, the sheep only has two forelegs.' He said: 'Hand me another foreleg.' He handed it to him and he ate it. Abu Ubayd said: 'That sheep had three forelegs that day.'
2801.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Ubayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia memasak periuk untuk Nabi ﷺ. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Berikan padaku kaki depannya.' Ia memberikannya. Beliau bersabda: 'Berikan lagi kaki depannya.' Ia berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, domba hanya punya dua kaki depan.' Beliau bersabda: 'Berikan lagi kaki depannya.' Ia memberikannya dan beliau memakannya. Abu Ubayd berkata: 'Domba itu memiliki tiga kaki depan pada hari itu.'

2802.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out to fight the polytheists. My father Abdullah said: O Jabir, do not grieve if I am killed, for I owe a debt and I have children. He was killed and the creditors took his property. I went to the Prophet ﷺ and told him about what had happened. He spoke to the creditors and they accepted half of what he owed. The Prophet ﷺ told me: Take a dried date and put it in the container of dates. He did so, and the container was filled and more came out from it, and all the debts were paid from it.'
2802.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ keluar untuk memerangi kaum musyrik. Ayahku Abdullah berkata: Wahai Jabir, jangan bersedih jika aku terbunuh, karena aku punya hutang dan aku punya anak. Ia terbunuh dan para pemberi hutang mengambil hartanya. Aku pergi kepada Nabi ﷺ dan memberitahunya apa yang terjadi. Beliau berbicara kepada para pemberi hutang dan mereka menerima setengah dari apa yang ia hutangkan. Nabi ﷺ memberitahuku: Ambil sebuah kurma kering dan letakkan di dalam wadah kurma. Aku melakukannya, dan wadah itu penuh dan kurma terus keluar darinya, dan semua hutang dibayar darinya.'

2803.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'Allah has favored Muhammad ﷺ above all the prophets and above the inhabitants of the heavens.' They said: 'In what way, O Ibn Abbas?' He said: 'Allah said to the prophets: I am taking a covenant from you — from every prophet He took a covenant — on behalf of Muhammad ﷺ, that if Muhammad is sent while you are alive you must believe in him and support him. Then He commanded the angels similarly: If Muhammad is sent during your time, you must believe in him and support him.'
2803.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Allah telah mengutamakan Muhammad ﷺ di atas semua nabi dan di atas para penghuni langit.' Mereka berkata: 'Dalam hal apa, wahai Ibnu Abbas?' Ia berkata: 'Allah berfirman kepada para nabi: Aku mengambil perjanjian dari kalian — dari setiap nabi Dia mengambil perjanjian — atas nama Muhammad ﷺ, bahwa jika Muhammad diutus ketika kalian masih hidup kalian harus beriman kepadanya dan mendukungnya. Kemudian Dia memerintahkan para malaikat dengan hal yang serupa: Jika Muhammad diutus pada zamanmu, kalian harus beriman kepadanya dan mendukungnya.'

2804.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'Some of the companions of the Prophet ﷺ were sitting waiting for him. He came out until when he drew near them, he heard them speaking. One of them said: How amazing — Allah chose Ibrahim as a close friend, and another said: And He spoke directly to Musa, and another: And Isa is the word of Allah and His spirit. Another said: Allah chose Adam. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came out and greeted them and said: I have heard your words and your wonder at Ibrahim being Allah's close friend — and so he is — and Musa being the one Allah spoke to directly — and so he is — and Isa being the spirit and word of Allah — and so he is — and Adam being chosen — and so he is. But know that I am the beloved of Allah and I do not say this out of pride, and I will be the first to intercede and the first whose intercession will be accepted, and I will be the first to move the rings of Paradise, and Allah will open it for me and I will enter it accompanied by the poor of the believers, and I do not say this out of pride.'
2804.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Beberapa sahabat Nabi ﷺ sedang duduk menunggu beliau. Beliau keluar hingga ketika mendekat mereka, beliau mendengar mereka berbicara. Salah seorang berkata: Sungguh menakjubkan — Allah memilih Ibrahim sebagai khalil (kekasih dekat), dan yang lain berkata: Dan Dia berbicara langsung kepada Musa, dan yang lain: Dan Isa adalah kalimat Allah dan ruh-Nya. Yang lain berkata: Allah memilih Adam. Rasulullah ﷺ keluar dan mengucapkan salam kepada mereka lalu bersabda: Aku telah mendengar perkataan kalian dan kekaguman kalian kepada Ibrahim sebagai khalilullah — dan demikianlah — dan Musa sebagai yang diajak bicara langsung oleh Allah — dan demikianlah — dan Isa sebagai ruh dan kalimat Allah — dan demikianlah — dan Adam sebagai yang dipilih — dan demikianlah. Namun ketahuilah bahwa akulah Habibullah (kekasih Allah) dan aku tidak mengatakannya dengan bangga, dan akulah yang pertama memberi syafaat dan yang pertama syafaatnya diterima, dan akulah yang pertama menggerak cincin-cincin Surga, dan Allah akan membukanya untukku dan aku akan memasukinya diiringi oleh orang-orang miskin dari kalangan orang beriman, dan aku tidak mengatakannya dengan bangga.'

2805.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I am the first of them to emerge from the grave, I am their leader when they arrive, I am their spokesman when they are speechless, I am their intercessor when they are held back — and I do not say this out of pride. I carry the banner of praise on the Day of Resurrection — and I do not say this out of pride.'
2805.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku adalah yang pertama dari mereka keluar dari kubur, aku adalah pemimpin mereka ketika mereka tiba, aku adalah juru bicara mereka ketika mereka tidak bisa berkata-kata, aku adalah pemberi syafaat mereka ketika mereka tertahan — dan aku tidak mengatakannya dengan bangga. Aku membawa panji pujian pada Hari Kebangkitan — dan aku tidak mengatakannya dengan bangga.'

2806.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I have been given five things that were not given to anyone before me: I was made victorious by awe at a distance of a month's journey; the entire earth has been made a place of prayer and purification for me; war booty has been made lawful for me while it was not lawful for anyone before me; I have been given the right of intercession; and a prophet used to be sent to his specific people alone, but I have been sent to all of humanity.'
2806.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku telah diberi lima hal yang tidak diberikan kepada siapapun sebelumku: Aku dimenangkan dengan rasa takut dari jarak perjalanan satu bulan; seluruh bumi dijadikan tempat shalat dan bersuci bagiku; rampasan perang dihalalkan bagiku sedangkan tidak dihalalkan bagi siapapun sebelumku; aku diberi hak syafaat; dan seorang nabi biasa diutus hanya kepada kaumnya secara khusus, tetapi aku diutus kepada seluruh umat manusia.'

2807.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'I am the first to take hold of the ring of the gate of Paradise and knock it.' Anas said: 'And I believe the Prophet ﷺ said: I will be the first to intercede.'
2807.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Akulah yang pertama memegang cincin pintu Surga dan mengetuknya.' Anas berkata: 'Dan aku percaya Nabi ﷺ juga bersabda: Akulah yang pertama memberi syafaat.'

2808.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I am the first intercessor in Paradise.'
2808.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Akulah pemberi syafaat pertama di Surga.'

2809.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: I am the first of people to be resurrected when the blowing of the Horn happens, and I will be at the gate of Paradise interceding while the other prophets are still in their shrouds — so I will be the first.'
2809.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Akulah yang pertama dari manusia dibangkitkan ketika tiupan Sangkakala terjadi, dan aku akan berada di pintu Surga memberi syafaat sementara para nabi lain masih dalam kain kafan mereka — maka akulah yang pertama.'

2810.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Ghanam that he said: 'Gabriel (peace be upon him) descended to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and opened his chest, then Gabriel said: O Muhammad, your Lord sends you greetings and says: I have chosen you above all My creation and I have honored you. So ask and you will be given.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'O Lord, You are One who has no partner, Ibrahim was Your close friend, Musa was the one You spoke to directly, and Isa was Your word and spirit — so what will I have?' Allah said: 'You are My beloved. And I do not say this out of pride.'
2810.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Ghanam bahwa ia berkata: 'Jibril (alaihissalam) turun kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan membuka dadanya, lalu Jibril berkata: Wahai Muhammad, Tuhanmu menyampaikan salam kepadamu dan berfirman: Aku telah memilihmu di atas seluruh makhluk-Ku dan telah memuliakanmu. Maka mintalah dan akan diberikan.' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ya Tuhan, Engkau Maha Esa yang tiada sekutu bagi-Mu, Ibrahim adalah khalil-Mu (kekasih dekat-Mu), Musa adalah yang Engkau ajak berbicara langsung, dan Isa adalah kalimat dan ruh-Mu — maka apa yang akan aku dapatkan?' Allah berfirman: 'Engkau adalah kekasih-Ku. Dan aku tidak mengatakannya dengan bangga.'

2811.iwh_en: It was narrated from Amr ibn Qays that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Allah reached with me the decreed term of mercy and made it concise for me. We are the last and the first — meaning we are the last of the nations but the first on the Day of Resurrection. And I am the leader of the prophets and their spokesman and the one whose intercession is not rejected, and I do not say this out of pride.'
2811.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Amr ibn Qais bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Allah mencapai bersamaku ajal yang penuh rahmat dan mempersingkatnya untukku. Kami adalah yang terakhir dan yang pertama — maksudnya kami adalah umat yang terakhir tetapi yang pertama pada Hari Kebangkitan. Dan akulah pemimpin para nabi dan juru bicara mereka dan yang syafaatnya tidak ditolak, dan aku tidak mengatakannya dengan bangga.'

2812.iwh_en: It was narrated from Maslamah al-Sakuni (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'While we were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he looked up at the sky and then looked down, then said: This is the time when knowledge will be lifted from the people until they are unable to do anything with it.' Ziyad ibn Labid al-Ansari said: 'O Messenger of Allah, how will knowledge be lifted when we have read the Quran? By Allah, we will teach it to our children and our children will teach it to their children until the Day of Resurrection.' He said: 'O Ziyad, I thought you were the most knowledgeable man in Madinah. This Torah and Gospel — are they not with the Jews and Christians? But do they benefit them at all?'
2812.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Maslamah al-Sakuni (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika kami bersama Rasulullah ﷺ, beliau melihat ke langit lalu menundukkan pandangan, kemudian bersabda: Inilah saat ketika ilmu akan diangkat dari manusia hingga mereka tidak mampu melakukan apa-apa dengannya.' Ziyad ibn Labid al-Anshari berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, bagaimana ilmu akan diangkat sementara kami telah membaca Al-Quran? Demi Allah, kami akan mengajarkannya kepada anak-anak kami dan anak-anak kami akan mengajarkannya kepada anak-anak mereka hingga Hari Kiamat.' Beliau bersabda: 'Wahai Ziyad, aku kira kamu adalah orang paling berilmu di Madinah. Taurat dan Injil ini — bukankah keduanya ada pada orang-orang Yahudi dan Nasrani? Namun apakah keduanya bermanfaat bagi mereka sama sekali?'

2813.iwh_en: It was narrated from Samurah ibn Jundub (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was brought a bowl of tharid (bread and meat stew). It was placed between him and his companions, and they ate from it. The people multiplied and came in relays throughout the day — yet the food remained as it was.
2813.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Samurah ibn Jundub (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ didatangkan sebuah mangkuk berisi tsarid (roti dan semur daging). Mangkuk itu diletakkan antara beliau dan para sahabatnya, dan mereka makan darinya. Orang-orang bertambah banyak dan datang bergantian sepanjang hari — namun makanan tetap seperti sediakala.

2814.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samurah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on a moonlit night wearing a red garment. I looked at him and then at the moon, and he was more beautiful to me than the moon.'
2814.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samurah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ pada malam terang bulan mengenakan pakaian merah. Aku memandangnya dan kemudian memandang bulan, dan ia lebih indah bagiku daripada bulan.'

2815.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ had a seal between his shoulder blades — like a large egg — and it was illuminated.'
2815.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ memiliki stempel kenabian di antara kedua bahunya — seperti telur besar — dan itu bercahaya.'

2816.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'I never saw anyone more helpful, more generous, more courageous, or more radiant and handsome than the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
2816.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak pernah melihat siapapun yang lebih suka menolong, lebih dermawan, lebih pemberani, atau lebih bercahaya dan tampan daripada Rasulullah ﷺ.'

2817.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Rubayyi' bint Mu'awwidh ibn Afra' (may Allah be pleased with her) that Abu Ubaydah ibn Muhammad ibn Ammar ibn Yasir said: 'I asked al-Rubayyi' bint Mu'awwidh ibn Afra': Describe for us the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. She said: O my son, if you had seen him, you would have seen the sun rising.'
2817.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Rubayyi' binti Mu'awwidh ibn Afra' (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Abu Ubaydah ibn Muhammad ibn Ammar ibn Yasir berkata: 'Aku meminta al-Rubayyi' binti Mu'awwidh ibn Afra': Gambarkanlah kepada kami Rasulullah ﷺ. Ia berkata: Wahai anakku, seandainya kamu melihatnya, kamu akan melihat matahari yang sedang terbit.'

2818.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ had a radiant complexion — as if his sweat were pearls. When he walked, he walked with a forward lean. I never touched silk or brocade softer than the palm of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and I never smelled musk or ambergris more fragrant than the scent of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
2818.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ berkulit cerah bercahaya — seakan keringatnya adalah mutiara. Ketika berjalan, beliau berjalan dengan sedikit condong ke depan. Aku tidak pernah menyentuh sutra atau brokat yang lebih lembut dari telapak tangan Rasulullah ﷺ, dan aku tidak pernah mencium minyak kasturi atau ambar yang lebih harum dari aroma Rasulullah ﷺ.'

2819.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I served the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he never once said "uff" to me, nor did he say about anything I had done: Why did you do such and such? Nor did he say about anything I had not done: Why did you not do such and such?'
2819.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melayani Rasulullah ﷺ dan beliau tidak pernah sekali pun berkata "uff" kepadaku, dan tidak pernah berkata tentang sesuatu yang aku lakukan: Mengapa kamu melakukan ini dan itu? Dan tidak pernah berkata tentang sesuatu yang tidak aku lakukan: Mengapa kamu tidak melakukan ini dan itu?'

2820.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man of the Banu Huraysh tribe that he said: 'I was with my father when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stoned Maiz ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him). When the stones struck him and he felt the pain, he fled. A man of the Ansar intercepted him and struck him with the jaw-bone of a camel and killed him. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ had a good opinion of him, but when the Prophet ﷺ was informed about how he had fled from the stones, he said: Why did you not leave him? Perhaps he would have repented and Allah would have accepted his repentance.'
2820.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang pria dari suku Banu Huraysh bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bersama ayahku ketika Rasulullah ﷺ merajam Ma'iz ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu). Ketika batu-batu mengenainya dan ia merasakan sakit, ia melarikan diri. Seorang pria dari Anshar mencegatnya dan memukulnya dengan rahang unta dan membunuhnya. Rasulullah ﷺ memiliki prasangka baik kepadanya, namun ketika Nabi ﷺ diberitahu tentang bagaimana ia melarikan diri dari batu-batu, beliau bersabda: Mengapa kalian tidak membiarkannya? Mungkin ia akan bertobat dan Allah akan menerima tobatnya.'

2821.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Bara' (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man asked him: 'Tell me — was the face of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ like a sword?' He said: 'No, like the moon.'
2821.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Bara' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang pria bertanya kepadanya: 'Ceritakanlah padaku — apakah wajah Rasulullah ﷺ seperti pedang?' Ia berkata: 'Tidak, seperti bulan.'

2822.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibrahim (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ could be recognized at night by the pleasant fragrance of his scent.
2822.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibrahim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ dapat dikenali di malam hari dari aroma harum tubuhnya.

2823.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ never walked along a path and then had someone follow him along the same path except that the person recognized him by the fragrance of his scent that lingered on the road.
2823.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ tidak pernah melewati suatu jalan kemudian seseorang mengikutinya di jalan yang sama kecuali orang tersebut mengenalinya dari aroma harum yang tertinggal di jalan itu.

2824.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Salamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to accept gifts but not accept charity. A woman from the Jews of Khaybar sent him a roasted sheep that had been poisoned. He took a bite of it and it spoke to him: I am poisoned. He spat it out and did not eat any more of it.'
2824.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Salamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa menerima hadiah tetapi tidak menerima sedekah. Seorang wanita dari orang-orang Yahudi Khaybar mengirimi beliau seekor domba panggang yang telah diberi racun. Beliau mengambil satu suapan darinya dan domba itu berbicara kepadanya: Aku telah diberi racun. Beliau memuntahkannya dan tidak lagi memakannya.'

2825.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'A Jewish woman from among the people of Khaybar poisoned a roasted sheep, then presented it as a gift to the Prophet ﷺ. He took some of it and ate it. When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ realized it was poisoned, he summoned the woman and asked her about it. She said: I wanted to kill you. He said: Allah would not have given you power over me to do that.'
2825.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Seorang wanita Yahudi dari penduduk Khaybar meracuni seekor domba panggang, kemudian mempersembahkannya sebagai hadiah kepada Nabi ﷺ. Beliau mengambil sebagian darinya dan memakannya. Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ menyadari itu beracun, beliau memanggil wanita itu dan menanyainya. Ia berkata: Aku ingin membunuhmu. Beliau bersabda: Allah tidak akan memberimu kekuasaan untuk melakukan itu terhadapku.'

2826.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'When Khaybar was conquered, a roasted sheep containing poison was presented to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ as a gift. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: Gather together all the Jews in Khaybar for me. They were gathered, and he said to them: I am going to ask you about something — will you tell me the truth? They said: Yes. He said: Who is your father? They said: So-and-so. He said: You have lied — your father is so-and-so. They said: You have spoken the truth. He then said: Will you tell me the truth about what I ask? They said: Yes, and if we lie, you already know how to identify a lie just as you knew our father. He said: Who are the people of the Fire? They said: We will be in it for a short time and then you will follow us. He said: You will remain in it disgraced. By Allah, we shall never follow you into it. Then he said: Will you tell me about this sheep — is it poisoned? They said: Yes. He said: What made you do that? They said: We wanted to know — if you were a liar, we would be rid of you; and if you were a prophet, it would not harm you.'
2826.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika Khaybar ditaklukkan, seekor domba panggang berisi racun dipersembahkan kepada Rasulullah ﷺ sebagai hadiah. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Kumpulkan semua orang Yahudi di Khaybar untukku. Mereka dikumpulkan, dan beliau berkata kepada mereka: Aku akan bertanya kepada kalian tentang sesuatu — akankah kalian memberiku jawaban yang jujur? Mereka berkata: Ya. Beliau berkata: Siapakah ayah kalian? Mereka berkata: Si fulan. Beliau berkata: Kalian berbohong — ayah kalian adalah si fulan. Mereka berkata: Kamu benar. Beliau kemudian berkata: Akankah kalian memberiku jawaban jujur tentang apa yang aku tanyakan? Mereka berkata: Ya, dan jika kami berbohong, kamu sudah tahu cara mengidentifikasi kebohongan sebagaimana kamu mengenali ayah kami. Beliau berkata: Siapa penghuni neraka? Mereka berkata: Kami akan ada di dalamnya untuk waktu singkat kemudian kalian akan menyusul kami. Beliau berkata: Kalian akan tinggal di dalamnya dengan hina. Demi Allah, kami tidak akan pernah mengikuti kalian ke dalamnya. Kemudian beliau berkata: Ceritakan kepadaku tentang domba ini — apakah itu beracun? Mereka berkata: Ya. Beliau berkata: Apa yang mendorong kalian melakukan itu? Mereka berkata: Kami ingin mengetahui — jika kamu seorang pembohong, kami akan terbebas darimu; dan jika kamu seorang nabi, itu tidak akan membahayakanmu.'

2827.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Prophet ﷺ was never asked for anything and said no.' Ibn Uyaynah commented: 'If he did not have it, he would promise it.'
2827.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ tidak pernah dimintai sesuatu lalu berkata tidak.' Ibnu Uyainah berkomentar: 'Jika beliau tidak memilikinya, beliau akan menjanjikannya.'

2828.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sahl ibn Sa'd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was modest and shy. He was never asked for anything except that he gave it.'
2828.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sahl ibn Sa'd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ adalah orang yang pemalu dan rendah hati. Beliau tidak pernah dimintai sesuatu kecuali beliau memberikannya.'

2829.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man of the Arabs that he said: 'I jostled with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on the Day of Hunayn. He was wearing a woolen garment that scratched me. I grabbed hold of his garment and said: O Messenger of Allah, give me from this wealth that Allah has given you. He turned to me and laughed, then commanded that something be given to me.'
2829.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang pria dari Arab bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berdesakan dengan Rasulullah ﷺ pada Hari Hunain. Beliau mengenakan jubah wol yang menggoresiku. Aku memegang jubahnya dan berkata: Ya Rasulullah, berikan kepadaku dari harta yang Allah telah berikan kepadamu. Beliau berbalik kepadaku dan tertawa, lalu memerintahkan agar sesuatu diberikan kepadaku.'

2830.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Zuhri that Gabriel said: 'I have searched through every household of ten people on earth, and I have found no one who spends this wealth more generously than the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
2830.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Zuhri bahwa Jibril berkata: 'Aku telah memeriksa setiap rumah tangga yang terdiri dari sepuluh orang di bumi, dan aku tidak menemukan siapapun yang menafkahkan harta ini dengan lebih dermawan dari Rasulullah ﷺ.'

2831.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Abi Awfa (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Prophet ﷺ used to remember Allah abundantly, engage in idle talk rarely, prolong his prayer, and shorten his sermon. He did not disdain to walk with a widow or a poor person until their need was fulfilled.'
2831.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Abi Awfa (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ biasa banyak berdzikir kepada Allah, jarang berbicara sia-sia, memperpanjang shalatnya, dan mempersingkat khutbahnya. Beliau tidak segan berjalan bersama seorang janda atau orang miskin sampai kebutuhan mereka terpenuhi.'

2832.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I wanted to know how long the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had left among us, so I said: O Messenger of Allah, I see them entering into Islam in great numbers — yet I used to think they would enter more slowly. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: Is there anything in this matter for you, O Abbas? The world will not end until you see something that will comfort you — then he recited this verse: When the victory of Allah has come and the conquest. (Quran 110:1) — until its end.'
2832.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku ingin mengetahui berapa lama lagi Rasulullah ﷺ akan berada bersama kami, maka aku berkata: Ya Rasulullah, aku melihat mereka masuk Islam dalam jumlah besar — namun dulu aku mengira mereka akan masuk lebih lambat. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Apakah ada sesuatu dalam perkara ini untukmu, wahai Abbas? Dunia tidak akan berakhir sampai kamu melihat sesuatu yang akan menghiburmu — kemudian beliau membaca ayat ini: Apabila telah datang pertolongan Allah dan kemenangan. (QS. An-Nashr: 1) — hingga akhirnya.'

2833.iwh_en: It was narrated from Dawud ibn Ali that it was said: 'O Messenger of Allah, shall we not provide you with a guard?' He said: 'No, let them step on my heels and I will step on theirs, until Allah gives me rest from them.'
2833.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Dawud ibn Ali bahwa dikatakan: 'Ya Rasulullah, bolehkah kami tidak menyediakan penjaga untukmu?' Beliau bersabda: 'Tidak, biarkan mereka menginjak tumitku dan aku menginjak tumit mereka, sampai Allah memberiku istirahat dari mereka.'

2834.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came out to us during the illness in which he died, and we were about three hundred men praying. He gestured with his hand over us and said: I give you glad tidings, I affirm to you that it is my prayer that is hoped for for you, and my intercession — and it is not only for those nearest to me. My greatest concern regarding you after I am gone — that doors of luxury will be opened to you and riches will multiply for you.'
2834.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ keluar menemui kami selama sakit yang menyebabkan kematiannya, dan kami berjumlah sekitar tiga ratus orang yang sedang shalat. Beliau mengisyaratkan tangannya ke arah kami dan bersabda: Aku memberi kalian kabar gembira, aku menegaskan kepada kalian bahwa shalatku diharapkan untuk kalian, dan syafaatku — dan itu bukan hanya untuk orang-orang yang paling dekat denganku. Kekhawatiranku yang terbesar mengenai kalian setelah aku pergi — bahwa pintu-pintu kemewahan akan terbuka untuk kalian dan kekayaan akan berlipat ganda bagi kalian.'

2835.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Muwayhiba (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to him in the middle of the night and said: 'O Abu Muwayhiba, I have been commanded to seek forgiveness for the people of this graveyard, so come with me.' He came with him. When he stood before them, the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Peace be upon you, O people of the graves. How blessed is your state compared to what the people face. Trials have come like dark portions of night, one following another, each worse than the last.' Then he turned to Abu Muwayhiba and said: 'I have been offered the keys of the world and immortality in it, followed by Paradise — or to meet my Lord and then Paradise. I chose to meet my Lord and Paradise.' Abu Muwayhiba said: 'May my father and mother be your ransom — take the keys of the world.' He said: 'No, O Abu Muwayhiba, I have chosen to meet my Lord.'
2835.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Muwayhiba (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mendatanginya di tengah malam dan bersabda: 'Wahai Abu Muwayhiba, aku diperintahkan untuk memohon ampun bagi penduduk pekuburan ini, maka ikutlah bersamaku.' Ia menemani beliau. Ketika berdiri di hadapan mereka, Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Salam sejahtera atas kalian, wahai penghuni kubur. Betapa beruntungnya keadaan kalian dibandingkan apa yang dihadapi manusia. Cobaan telah datang seperti kegelapan malam, satu demi satu, masing-masing lebih buruk dari sebelumnya.' Kemudian beliau berbalik kepada Abu Muwayhiba dan bersabda: 'Aku ditawari kunci-kunci dunia dan keabadian di dalamnya, lalu Surga — atau bertemu Tuhanku lalu Surga. Aku memilih untuk bertemu Tuhanku dan Surga.' Abu Muwayhiba berkata: 'Semoga ayah dan ibuku menjadi tebusanmu — ambillah kunci-kunci dunia.' Beliau bersabda: 'Tidak, wahai Abu Muwayhiba, aku telah memilih untuk bertemu Tuhanku.'

2836.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that he said: 'When the verse was revealed: When the victory of Allah has come and the conquest (Quran 110:1), the Messenger of Allah ﷺ called Fatimah and said: My death has been announced to me. She wept. He said: Do not cry, for you will be the first of my family to follow me. She laughed.'
2836.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika turun ayat: Apabila telah datang pertolongan Allah dan kemenangan (QS. An-Nashr: 1), Rasulullah ﷺ memanggil Fatimah dan bersabda: Kematianku telah diumumkan kepadaku. Ia menangis. Beliau bersabda: Jangan menangis, karena kamu akan menjadi orang pertama dari keluargaku yang mengikutiku. Ia pun tersenyum.'

2837.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Prophet ﷺ returned to me from the mosque while a group of women from the Ansar were with me. He entered and drew a curtain between himself and the women, then asked: How are you? They said: Good, may Allah show you good, O Messenger of Allah. His face appeared pale, and he said: I have heard that you fear for me in my illness. Are you afraid that I might go to other than you? It was said: We are afraid of that. He said: O Allah, forgive the women of the Ansar. Aisha said: They left and the Prophet ﷺ became gravely ill.'
2837.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisha (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ kembali kepadaku dari masjid sementara sekelompok wanita dari Anshar bersamaku. Beliau masuk dan menarik tirai antara dirinya dan wanita-wanita itu, lalu bertanya: Bagaimana keadaan kalian? Mereka berkata: Baik, semoga Allah menunjukkan kebaikan kepadamu, wahai Rasulullah. Wajahnya tampak pucat, dan beliau bersabda: Aku mendengar bahwa kalian khawatir tentangku dalam sakitku. Apakah kalian takut bahwa aku mungkin pergi ke selain kalian? Dikatakan: Kami khawatir tentang itu. Beliau bersabda: Ya Allah, ampunilah wanita-wanita Anshar. Aisha berkata: Mereka pergi dan Nabi ﷺ jatuh sakit parah.'

2838.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Prophet ﷺ said during his illness: Pour over me seven waterskins whose ties have not been untied, so that I may go out to the people and advise them.' She said: 'We seated him in a large tub and poured water over him until he signaled to us to stop.'
2838.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisha (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ bersabda selama sakitnya: Tuangkan atas diriku tujuh kantong air yang ikatannya belum dibuka, agar aku dapat keluar kepada manusia dan menasihati mereka.' Ia berkata: 'Kami mendudukkan beliau di dalam bak besar dan menuangkan air ke atasnya hingga beliau memberi isyarat kepada kami untuk berhenti.'

2839.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was called to prayer during his illness and said: Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in prayer. I said: O Messenger of Allah, Abu Bakr is a tender-hearted man — when he stands in your place, he will not be heard by the people due to weeping. He said: Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in prayer. And so Abu Bakr led the people in prayer during the lifetime of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
2839.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisha (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ dipanggil untuk shalat selama sakitnya dan bersabda: Perintahkan Abu Bakr untuk memimpin orang-orang shalat. Aku berkata: Ya Rasulullah, Abu Bakr adalah orang yang lembut hatinya — ketika berdiri di tempatmu, dia tidak akan terdengar oleh orang-orang karena tangisannya. Beliau bersabda: Perintahkan Abu Bakr untuk memimpin orang-orang shalat. Dan demikianlah Abu Bakr memimpin orang-orang shalat semasa hidupnya Rasulullah ﷺ.'

2840.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ikrimah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ passed away on a Monday. His body remained for the rest of that day, through the night and the following day until he was buried on the night of Wednesday. Some said: Should we not bury him? Others said: Do not bury him until more people can attend.'
2840.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ikrimah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ wafat pada hari Senin. Jasadnya dibiarkan untuk sisa hari itu, sepanjang malam dan keesokan harinya hingga dikubur pada malam Rabu. Sebagian berkata: Tidakkah kita menguburnya? Yang lain berkata: Jangan kuburkan hingga lebih banyak orang dapat hadir.'

2841.iwh_en: It was narrated from Makhul that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'When one of you is struck by an affliction, let him remember his affliction because of me, for it is one of the greatest of afflictions.'
2841.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Makhul bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Ketika salah seorang dari kalian ditimpa musibah, hendaklah ia mengingat musibahnya karena aku, karena itu adalah salah satu musibah yang paling besar.'

2842.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ata' (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When one of you is struck by an affliction, let him remember his affliction because of me, for it is one of the greatest of afflictions.'
2842.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Atha' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ketika salah seorang dari kalian ditimpa musibah, hendaklah ia mengingat musibahnya karena aku, karena itu adalah salah satu musibah yang paling besar.'

2843.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umar ibn Muhammad that he said: 'I never heard Ibn Umar mention the Prophet ﷺ without weeping.'
2843.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umar ibn Muhammad bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku tidak pernah mendengar Ibnu Umar menyebut Nabi ﷺ tanpa menangis.'

2844.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that Fatimah (may Allah be pleased with her) said: 'O Anas, how did your souls allow you to pour dirt over the Messenger of Allah ﷺ?' She said: 'O my father, from your Lord who called you; O my father, who responded to the One who invited him; O my father, his abode is the Garden of Paradise; O my father, we announce your death to Gabriel.'
2844.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Fatimah (radhiyallahu 'anha) berkata: 'Wahai Anas, bagaimana jiwa-jiwa kalian mengizinkan kalian menaburkan tanah di atas Rasulullah ﷺ?' Ia berkata: 'Wahai ayahku, dari Tuhanmu yang memanggilmu; Wahai ayahku, yang menyambut seruan Sang Pengundang; Wahai ayahku, tempat tinggalnya adalah Surga Firdaus; Wahai ayahku, kami mengumumkan kematianmu kepada Jibril.'

2845.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that he mentioned the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'I was present on the day he entered Madinah, and I never saw a day more beautiful or more radiant than the day on which the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to us. And I was present on the day he died, and I never saw a day more hideous or darker than the day on which the Messenger of Allah ﷺ died.'
2845.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia menyebut Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Aku hadir pada hari beliau memasuki Madinah, dan aku tidak pernah melihat hari yang lebih indah atau lebih cerah dari hari ketika Rasulullah ﷺ datang kepada kami. Dan aku hadir pada hari beliau wafat, dan aku tidak pernah melihat hari yang lebih buruk atau lebih gelap dari hari ketika Rasulullah ﷺ wafat.'

2846.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Salam (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said to the Prophet ﷺ: 'O Messenger of Allah, we find you on the Day of Resurrection standing at the Pool.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Yes.' He said: 'And I find in the Torah that whoever drinks from it will never be thirsty.' He said: 'Yes. Whoever drinks from it will never be thirsty after that, and I will be the first to drink from it.'
2846.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Salam (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata kepada Nabi ﷺ: 'Ya Rasulullah, kami mendapati bahwa kamu pada Hari Kiamat berdiri di Telaga.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Ya.' Ia berkata: 'Dan aku mendapati dalam Taurat bahwa siapapun yang meminumnya tidak akan pernah haus.' Beliau bersabda: 'Ya. Siapapun yang meminumnya tidak akan pernah haus setelah itu, dan akulah yang pertama meminumnya.'

2847.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) through Abu Qurrah, the freed slave of Abu Jahl, that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'When this surah was revealed — When the victory of Allah has come and the conquest (Quran 110:1) — I said: The pillar of my Lord has departed. My death has approached.'
2847.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Abu Qurrah, mantan budak Abu Jahal, bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Ketika surah ini diturunkan — Apabila telah datang pertolongan Allah dan kemenangan (QS. An-Nashr: 1) — aku berkata: Tiang penopang Tuhanku telah pergi. Kematianku telah mendekat.'
